title: hyssopus officinalis subsp. angustifolius
intro: Hyssopus officinalis subsp. angustifolius, commonly known as narrow-leaved hyssop, is a subspecies of the perennial herb Hyssopus officinalis. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties due to the presence of essential oils and phenolic compounds. The health benefits of narrow-leaved hyssop include relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in digestion and reduce fever. Narrow-leaved hyssop can be prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and it is also used as a garnish or added to salads for its flavor and medicinal properties.
